summary_one_line: A long homogeneous run of one Khmer-category byte drives the syllable machine into the case-3 broken-cluster transition action.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose bytes map (after cmap) to a sustained run of a single khmer_category, long enough to drive the Ragel khmer state machine to a transition whose action is case 3 (te=p; p--; found_syllable(khmer_broken_cluster)). No specific magic constant or comparison operand is needed; the gate is a multi-glyph structural repetition, not a value match.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive havoc/token seeds contain long contiguous identical-byte runs (e.g. a block of 0x7c '|' bytes spanning offsets ~0x1c-0x2d in winning seeds 1/2, and 0x99/0x61 runs in seed 5), exactly the homogeneous category stream that walks the state machine into the case-3 broken-cluster action; the winner takes case 3 True=12 times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog seeds are short (40-76 bytes) and lack the long homogeneous category runs; their bytes are scattered/punctuated (e.g. 0xe2 17 00 00 patterns, mixed spaces and OTTO/kern tokens), so the loser never reaches the case-3 transition (case 3 True=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Naive's plain havoc/token mutation stack stumbles onto and extends long uniform byte runs (BytesRandSetMutator, BytesRandInsertMutator copy/repeat operators), which is what this structural gate needs. Cmplog's distinguishing input-to-state operand-substitution stage targets value-equality CMP operands; the case-3 gate is a Ragel key-span range check over repeated categories, not a discrete constant, so operand substitution has no equality to solve and instead spends mutation energy rewriting CMP operands that break the homogeneous run — the comparison-solving technique is the differentiator that HURTS cmplog, so the winning technique is naive havoc-only mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive winning over cmplog with the I2S technique delta.
The blocked side is the case-3 transition action that emits a broken-cluster syllable.
Case 3's body sets te/p and calls found_syllable for a broken cluster, so reaching it requires the state machine to walk into that transition.
The transition is selected by a key-span range check over the per-glyph khmer_category, i.e. a structural range gate not a discrete constant comparison.
The winner takes case 3 True=12 while the loser takes it True=0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 369  T=12 F=169  T=0 F=177  case 3:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning naive seeds contain a long contiguous run of the 0x7c byte.
Another winning naive seed contains a long contiguous run of repeated bytes (0x99 and 0x61).
Losing cmplog seeds are short and punctuated rather than long homogeneous runs.
The winning seeds were produced by naive havoc mutators including byte-set and byte-insert operators.
Cmplog's distinguishing mutator is I2SRandReplace, which substitutes CMP operands found in the CmpLog buffer.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Naive uses only the havoc + token mutator stack with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: This is an I2S-ANTI branch: the winner is naive, which has no I2SRandReplace, so the winning mechanism is havoc-only and no I2S lineage check is mandated. The case-3 gate is a Ragel key-span range check over repeated khmer_category values (SOURCE CONTEXT L334-336), not a discrete-constant CMP, so I2SRandReplace has no equality operand to solve; cmplog spending energy on operand substitution disrupts the long homogeneous category runs that naive's byte-set/insert operators build, which is why cmplog loses (case 3 True=0 vs winner True=12).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the case-3 transition is reached only by injecting a specific discrete magic constant (a single equality compare) and cmplog then resolves it faster than naive — that would show the gate is an I2S-solvable comparison, contradicting the structural-run hypothesis.

weakest_evidence_point: I infer the long byte runs map to a sustained single khmer_category from the source range-check shape, but the cmap-to-category mapping is not shown in the prompt, so the exact category of the 0x7c/0x99/0x61 runs is unverified.
